Source: vignettes/chapter-07-inference-part-2.qmd

Chapter 7: Inference, Part II

Fixed-Effects Inference in Linear Mixed Models

Muhammad Yaseen

May 17, 2026

library(modernGLMM)
library(emmeans)
library(car)

1 Overview

Chapter 7 covers fixed-effects inference: constructing estimable functions, computing contrasts, and performing multiple comparisons in (G)LMMs.

Key topics:

  • Estimability: not all linear combinations of \(\boldsymbol{\beta}\) can be estimated; emmeans handles this transparently.
  • Contrasts: custom linear combinations via emmeans::contrast().
  • Multiple comparisons: Tukey, Bonferroni, Scheffé, and FDR adjustments.
  • Interaction dissection: emmeans::joint_tests() for simple effects.

2 Treatment Structure Example (DataSet8.1)

The 2×3 factorial from Chapter 8 is used here to illustrate contrast coding.

data(DataSet8.1)
DataSet8.1$a <- factor(DataSet8.1$a)
DataSet8.1$b <- factor(DataSet8.1$b)

fit <- stats::lm(y ~ a * b, data = DataSet8.1)

2.1 Estimated marginal means 

emm <- emmeans::emmeans(fit, ~ a * b)
print(emm)
 a b emmean   SE df lower.CL upper.CL
 0 0   50.5 2.56 18     45.1     55.9
 1 0   67.8 2.56 18     62.4     73.1
 0 1   59.8 2.56 18     54.4     65.1
 1 1   67.2 2.56 18     61.9     72.6
 0 2   71.5 2.56 18     66.1     76.9
 1 2   71.0 2.56 18     65.6     76.4

Confidence level used: 0.95

2.2 All pairwise comparisons within each level of a 

pairs(
  emmeans::emmeans(fit, ~ b | a),
  adjust = "tukey"
)
a = 0:
 contrast estimate   SE df t.ratio p.value
 b0 - b1     -9.25 3.62 18  -2.554  0.0498
 b0 - b2    -21.00 3.62 18  -5.798 <0.0001
 b1 - b2    -11.75 3.62 18  -3.244  0.0119

a = 1:
 contrast estimate   SE df t.ratio p.value
 b0 - b1      0.50 3.62 18   0.138  0.9896
 b0 - b2     -3.25 3.62 18  -0.897  0.6488
 b1 - b2     -3.75 3.62 18  -1.035  0.5649

P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates

2.3 Simple effects (ANOVA for b within each level of a) 

emmeans::joint_tests(fit, by = "a")
model term a df1 df2 F.ratio p.value
1 b 0 2 18 16.888 0.0000742
3 b 1 2 18 0.632 0.5428773

2.4 Custom contrasts 

emmeans::contrast(emm, list(
  "a0: b0 vs b1" = c(1, -1, 0, 0, 0, 0),
  "a1: b0 vs b1" = c(0, 0, 0, 1, -1, 0)
))
 contrast     estimate   SE df t.ratio p.value
 a0: b0 vs b1   -17.25 3.62 18  -4.763  0.0002
 a1: b0 vs b1    -4.25 3.62 18  -1.173  0.2559

3 Key Takeaways

  • emmeans::emmeans() computes estimable marginal means automatically.
  • emmeans::contrast() accepts named lists of contrast vectors.
  • emmeans::joint_tests() provides F-tests for each term, optionally by a conditioning variable (equivalent to SAS SLICE).
  • Multiplicity adjustment (adjust = "tukey", "bonferroni", "fdr") is passed as an argument to pairs() or contrast().

4 References

Stroup, W. W., Ptukhina, M., and Garai, S. (2024). Generalized Linear Mixed Models: Modern Concepts, Methods and Applications (2nd ed.). CRC Press.

library(modernGLMM)
library(emmeans)
library(car)

1 Overview

Chapter 7 covers fixed-effects inference: constructing estimable functions, computing contrasts, and performing multiple comparisons in (G)LMMs.

Key topics:

  • Estimability: not all linear combinations of \(\boldsymbol{\beta}\) can be estimated; emmeans handles this transparently.
  • Contrasts: custom linear combinations via emmeans::contrast().
  • Multiple comparisons: Tukey, Bonferroni, Scheffé, and FDR adjustments.
  • Interaction dissection: emmeans::joint_tests() for simple effects.

2 Treatment Structure Example (DataSet8.1)

The 2×3 factorial from Chapter 8 is used here to illustrate contrast coding.

data(DataSet8.1)
DataSet8.1$a <- factor(DataSet8.1$a)
DataSet8.1$b <- factor(DataSet8.1$b)

fit <- stats::lm(y ~ a * b, data = DataSet8.1)

2.1 Estimated marginal means 

emm <- emmeans::emmeans(fit, ~ a * b)
print(emm)
 a b emmean   SE df lower.CL upper.CL
 0 0   50.5 2.56 18     45.1     55.9
 1 0   67.8 2.56 18     62.4     73.1
 0 1   59.8 2.56 18     54.4     65.1
 1 1   67.2 2.56 18     61.9     72.6
 0 2   71.5 2.56 18     66.1     76.9
 1 2   71.0 2.56 18     65.6     76.4

Confidence level used: 0.95

2.2 All pairwise comparisons within each level of a 

pairs(
  emmeans::emmeans(fit, ~ b | a),
  adjust = "tukey"
)
a = 0:
 contrast estimate   SE df t.ratio p.value
 b0 - b1     -9.25 3.62 18  -2.554  0.0498
 b0 - b2    -21.00 3.62 18  -5.798 <0.0001
 b1 - b2    -11.75 3.62 18  -3.244  0.0119

a = 1:
 contrast estimate   SE df t.ratio p.value
 b0 - b1      0.50 3.62 18   0.138  0.9896
 b0 - b2     -3.25 3.62 18  -0.897  0.6488
 b1 - b2     -3.75 3.62 18  -1.035  0.5649

P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates

2.3 Simple effects (ANOVA for b within each level of a) 

emmeans::joint_tests(fit, by = "a")
model term a df1 df2 F.ratio p.value
1 b 0 2 18 16.888 0.0000742
3 b 1 2 18 0.632 0.5428773

2.4 Custom contrasts 

emmeans::contrast(emm, list(
  "a0: b0 vs b1" = c(1, -1, 0, 0, 0, 0),
  "a1: b0 vs b1" = c(0, 0, 0, 1, -1, 0)
))
 contrast     estimate   SE df t.ratio p.value
 a0: b0 vs b1   -17.25 3.62 18  -4.763  0.0002
 a1: b0 vs b1    -4.25 3.62 18  -1.173  0.2559

3 Key Takeaways

  • emmeans::emmeans() computes estimable marginal means automatically.
  • emmeans::contrast() accepts named lists of contrast vectors.
  • emmeans::joint_tests() provides F-tests for each term, optionally by a conditioning variable (equivalent to SAS SLICE).
  • Multiplicity adjustment (adjust = "tukey", "bonferroni", "fdr") is passed as an argument to pairs() or contrast().

4 References

Stroup, W. W., Ptukhina, M., and Garai, S. (2024). Generalized Linear Mixed Models: Modern Concepts, Methods and Applications (2nd ed.). CRC Press.

On this page

Developed by Muhammad Yaseen, Adeela Munawar, Walter W. Stroup, Marina Ptukhina, Soumit Garai.
